maml:description.maml:para: Adds a message to an existing conversation.
Appends a new message to a conversation's Messages array. The conversation is modified in place.
The message content.
The conversation hashtable created by New-AnthropicConversation.
Return the updated conversation object.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The role of the message sender ('user' or 'assistant').
The conversation hashtable created by New-AnthropicConversation.
Return the updated conversation object.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Alternative: Add the text from an API response as an assistant message.
The message content.
The conversation hashtable created by New-AnthropicConversation.
Return the updated conversation object.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Alternative: Add the text from an API response as an assistant message.
The role of the message sender ('user' or 'assistant').

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-AnthropicMessage -Conversation $conv -Role 'user' -Content 'Hello!'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Add-AnthropicMessage -Conversation $conv -Response $response # Adds the assistant's response text to the conversation

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $conv = $conv | Add-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'Hi' -PassThru

maml:description.maml:para: Clears the router configuration and disables routing.
Removes the router configuration, disabling automatic model routing. After clearing, Invoke-AnthropicRouted will fail until Set-AnthropicRouterConfig is called again.
Clear without confirmation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Clear without confirmation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Clear-AnthropicRouterConfig

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Clear-AnthropicRouterConfig -Force

maml:description.maml:para: Clears the cached runspaces used by safe command execution.
The PSAnthropic module caches constrained runspaces for performance when executing tool commands. This function disposes all cached runspaces and clears the cache.
Call this function when: - You want to reclaim memory from cached runspaces
- You need to reset the execution environment
- Before unloading the module in long-running sessions
Note: The module automatically cleans up runspaces when it is removed via Remove-Module. This function is for manual cleanup during active use.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Clear-AnthropicRunspaceCache

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Clean up after intensive tool usage $tools = Get-AnthropicStandardTools # ... execute many tool calls ... Clear-AnthropicRunspaceCache

dev:remarks.maml:para: Explicitly reclaims memory after heavy tool usage.

maml:description.maml:para: Establishes a connection to an Anthropic-compatible API endpoint.
Initializes the connection settings for communicating with Ollama's Anthropic-compatible API. Stores connection info in a script-scoped variable.

Parameters are checked in order: 1. Explicit parameters 2. Environment variables (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_MODEL) 3. Auto-detection (queries server for available models) 4. Defaults (localhost:11434, "ollama", "llama3")
The server address (e.g., 'localhost:11434' or 'http://localhost:11434'). Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or 'localhost:11434'.
The API key for authentication. For Ollama, use 'ollama' (accepted but not validated). Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or 'ollama'.
The default model to use for requests. Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_MODEL, then auto-detects from server, then 'llama3'.
Reconnect even if already connected.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The API key for authentication. For Ollama, use 'ollama' (accepted but not validated). Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or 'ollama'.
Reconnect even if already connected.
The default model to use for requests. Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_MODEL, then auto-detects from server, then 'llama3'.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The server address (e.g., 'localhost:11434' or 'http://localhost:11434'). Defaults to $env:ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or 'localhost:11434'.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Connect-Anthropic # Connects with defaults (localhost:11434, ollama, llama3)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Connect-Anthropic -Server 'localhost:11434' -Model 'qwen3-coder' # Connects with a specific model

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Connect-Anthropic -Server 'api.anthropic.com' -ApiKey $realApiKey -Model 'claude-3-5-sonnet' # Connects to Anthropic's cloud API (if needed)

maml:description.maml:para: Clears the current Anthropic API connection.
Removes the stored connection settings, requiring a new Connect-Anthropic call before making further API requests.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Disconnect-Anthropic # Clears the connection

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Disconnect-Anthropic -WhatIf # Shows what would happen without disconnecting

maml:description.maml:para: Shows the current Anthropic API connection settings.
Returns information about the current connection, including server, model, and when the connection was established. Does not expose the API key for security.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicConnection

dev:remarks.maml:para: Server : localhost:11434 Model : llama3 ConnectedAt : 1/23/2026 10:30:00 AM

maml:description.maml:para: Lists available models from the Ollama server.
Queries the Ollama /api/tags endpoint to list all available models. Note: This is Ollama-specific and won't work with Anthropic's cloud API.
Optional filter string to match model names.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional filter string to match model names.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicModel # Lists all available models

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicModel -Filter 'llama' # Lists models containing 'llama' in the name

maml:description.maml:para: Extracts text content from an Anthropic API response.
Convenience function to extract the text from a response object. Handles responses with multiple content blocks by concatenating all text.
The response object from Invoke-AnthropicMessage.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The response object from Invoke-AnthropicMessage.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $response = Invoke-AnthropicMessage -Messages @(New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'Hello') $response | Get-AnthropicResponseText

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicResponseText -Response $response

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the current router configuration.
Returns the current model routing configuration and logging settings.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicRouterConfig

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Check if router is configured if (Get-AnthropicRouterConfig) { "Router ready" }

maml:description.maml:para: Reads and analyzes the router log.
Retrieves routing decisions from the log file with optional filtering and statistics.
Path to the log file. If not specified, uses the configured LogPath.
Return only the last N entries.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Return summary statistics instead of raw log entries.
Return only the last N entries.
Path to the log file. If not specified, uses the configured LogPath.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Return summary statistics instead of raw log entries.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicRouterLog -Last 10

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-AnthropicRouterLog -Summary

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Get stats programmatically $stats = Get-AnthropicRouterLog -Summary $stats.ByModel['qwen3-coder-8k'] # Count for this model

maml:description.maml:para: Returns a set of standard tools for common operations.
Provides pre-defined tools similar to Anthropic's built-in tools: - pwsh: Execute PowerShell commands (like bash_20241022)
- str_replace_editor: Text editor with view/create/replace (like text_editor_20250124)
- read_file: Read file contents
- list_directory: List directory contents
- search_files: Search for files by pattern
- search_content: Search for text within files
- get_current_time: Get current date/time
- web_fetch: Fetch and parse content from URLs
Which set of tools to return: 'All', 'FileSystem', 'Editor', 'Shell'
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Which set of tools to return: 'All', 'FileSystem', 'Editor', 'Shell'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $tools = Get-AnthropicStandardTools Invoke-AnthropicMessage -Messages $messages -Tools $tools

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $tools = Get-AnthropicStandardTools -ToolSet FileSystem

maml:description.maml:para: Sends messages to the Anthropic Messages API (POST /v1/messages).
The primary function for interacting with Ollama's Anthropic-compatible API. Sends a conversation and returns the assistant's response.

Array of message hashtables or objects. Each message should have 'role' and 'content' keys. Use New-AnthropicMessage to create properly formatted messages. Supports pipeline input - messages are accumulated before the API call is made.
Maximum tokens to generate. Defaults to 4096.
The model to use. Defaults to the model set in Connect-Anthropic.
Context window size (Ollama-specific). Smaller values use less VRAM. Common values: 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768. Default is model-specific.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Array of strings that will stop generation when encountered.
Enable streaming output. Returns events as they arrive.
System prompt to set context for the conversation.
Sampling temperature (0.0-1.0). Lower values are more deterministic.
Enable extended thinking mode. The model will include its reasoning process.
Maximum tokens for the thinking process (requires -Thinking).
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
How to handle tool selection ('auto', 'any', 'tool', or specific tool name).
Array of tool definitions for function calling.
Sample from top K options for each token.
Nucleus sampling probability threshold.
Maximum tokens to generate. Defaults to 4096.

Array of message hashtables or objects. Each message should have 'role' and 'content' keys. Use New-AnthropicMessage to create properly formatted messages. Supports pipeline input - messages are accumulated before the API call is made.
The model to use. Defaults to the model set in Connect-Anthropic.
Context window size (Ollama-specific). Smaller values use less VRAM. Common values: 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768. Default is model-specific.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Array of strings that will stop generation when encountered.
Enable streaming output. Returns events as they arrive.
System prompt to set context for the conversation.
Sampling temperature (0.0-1.0). Lower values are more deterministic.
Enable extended thinking mode. The model will include its reasoning process.
Maximum tokens for the thinking process (requires -Thinking).
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
How to handle tool selection ('auto', 'any', 'tool', or specific tool name).
Array of tool definitions for function calling.
Sample from top K options for each token.
Nucleus sampling probability threshold.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $response = Invoke-AnthropicMessage -Messages @( New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'What is PowerShell?' ) $response | Get-AnthropicResponseText

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # With system prompt $response = Invoke-AnthropicMessage -Messages @( New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'Explain recursion' ) -System 'You are a programming tutor. Be concise.'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Pipeline input from conversation $conversation.Messages | Invoke-AnthropicMessage

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Pipeline with multiple messages @( New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'Hello' New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'assistant' -Content 'Hi there!' New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'How are you?' ) | Invoke-AnthropicMessage

maml:description.maml:para: Sends messages using automatic model routing based on task type.
Routes requests to the appropriate model based on TaskType parameter. Logs routing decisions for later analysis.
Requires Set-AnthropicRouterConfig to be called first.
Array of message objects. Use New-AnthropicMessage to create. Supports pipeline input - messages are accumulated before routing.
Maximum tokens to generate. Defaults to 4096.
Context window size (Ollama-specific).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Enable streaming output.
System prompt to set context for the conversation.
The type of task to route. Maps to configured models. Common types: Default, Code, Vision, Complex, Fast, Creative. If not specified, uses 'Default'.
Sampling temperature (0.0-1.0).
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
{{ Fill ToolChoice Description }}
Array of tool definitions for function calling.
Maximum tokens to generate. Defaults to 4096.
Array of message objects. Use New-AnthropicMessage to create. Supports pipeline input - messages are accumulated before routing.
Context window size (Ollama-specific).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Enable streaming output.
System prompt to set context for the conversation.
The type of task to route. Maps to configured models. Common types: Default, Code, Vision, Complex, Fast, Creative. If not specified, uses 'Default'.
Sampling temperature (0.0-1.0).
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
{{ Fill ToolChoice Description }}
Array of tool definitions for function calling.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Route to default model Invoke-AnthropicRouted -Messages $msgs

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Route to code model Invoke-AnthropicRouted -Messages $msgs -TaskType Code

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Route with tools (auto-selects code model if configured) Invoke-AnthropicRouted -Messages $msgs -TaskType Code -Tools $tools

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Pipeline input from conversation $conversation.Messages | Invoke-AnthropicRouted -TaskType Code

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a standard tool based on a tool_use response from the model.
Takes a tool_use object from the model's response and executes the corresponding standard tool, returning the result as a string.
Shell commands (pwsh tool) are executed in an isolated, constrained runspace for security. This prevents code injection, .NET type abuse, and restricts available commands to a curated safe list.
The tool_use object from the model's response containing name and input.
Controls how tool execution is handled: - Auto: Execute tools automatically without prompting (default)
- Confirm: Prompt user for confirmation before each tool execution
- None: Do not execute tools, return description of what would be executed
Maximum execution time for shell commands in seconds. Default is 30.
Maximum length of output to return (default: 10000 characters).
Array of allowed root directories for file operations. Paths outside these directories will be rejected. Defaults to current directory for security. Use -AllowAllPaths to disable path restrictions.
Maximum file size in bytes for read operations. Default is 10MB.
Maximum directory recursion depth for search operations. Default is 10.
Disables path restrictions, allowing file operations on any accessible path. Use with caution - this allows the model to read/write files anywhere.
Allow shell command execution. Default is $false for safety.
Allow web fetch operations. Default is $false for safety.
Allow write operations (create, str_replace, insert). Default is $false for safety.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
WARNING: Disables all sandboxing for shell commands, using Invoke-Expression directly. Only use for testing in controlled environments where you trust all input.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Disables path restrictions, allowing file operations on any accessible path. Use with caution - this allows the model to read/write files anywhere.
Array of allowed root directories for file operations. Paths outside these directories will be rejected. Defaults to current directory for security. Use -AllowAllPaths to disable path restrictions.
Allow shell command execution. Default is $false for safety.
Allow web fetch operations. Default is $false for safety.
Allow write operations (create, str_replace, insert). Default is $false for safety.
Controls how tool execution is handled: - Auto: Execute tools automatically without prompting (default)
- Confirm: Prompt user for confirmation before each tool execution
- None: Do not execute tools, return description of what would be executed
Maximum file size in bytes for read operations. Default is 10MB.
Maximum length of output to return (default: 10000 characters).
Maximum directory recursion depth for search operations. Default is 10.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Maximum execution time for shell commands in seconds. Default is 30.
The tool_use object from the model's response containing name and input.
WARNING: Disables all sandboxing for shell commands, using Invoke-Expression directly. Only use for testing in controlled environments where you trust all input.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $result = Invoke-AnthropicStandardTool -ToolUse $toolUse -AllowShell

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # With user confirmation before each execution $result = Invoke-AnthropicStandardTool -ToolUse $toolUse -AllowShell -InvokeMode Confirm

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Dry-run mode - see what would be executed without running it $result = Invoke-AnthropicStandardTool -ToolUse $toolUse -AllowShell -InvokeMode None

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # UNSAFE: No restrictions - for testing only! $result = Invoke-AnthropicStandardTool -ToolUse $toolUse -AllowShell -Unsafe

maml:description.maml:para: Core HTTP handler for Anthropic API requests.
Handles all HTTP communication with the Anthropic-compatible API. Includes connection validation, header injection, error handling, and retry logic for transient failures.
The full URI to request.
The HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.). Defaults to GET.
The request body (will be converted to JSON if hashtable).
The content type. Defaults to 'application/json'.
Additional headers to include (merged with connection headers).
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
Maximum retry attempts for 5xx errors. Defaults to 3.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The request body (will be converted to JSON if hashtable).
The content type. Defaults to 'application/json'.
Additional headers to include (merged with connection headers).
Maximum retry attempts for 5xx errors. Defaults to 3.
The HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.). Defaults to GET.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300.
The full URI to request.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Invoke-AnthropicWebRequest -Uri 'http://localhost:11434/v1/messages' -Method POST -Body $body

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new conversation object for multi-turn interactions.
Initializes a conversation hashtable with a Messages array and optional SystemPrompt. Use Add-AnthropicMessage to add messages to the conversation.
Optional initial user message to start the conversation.
Optional system prompt to set the assistant's behavior.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional system prompt to set the assistant's behavior.
Optional initial user message to start the conversation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $conv = New-AnthropicConversation -UserMessage 'Hello!' -SystemPrompt 'You are helpful.' $response = Invoke-AnthropicMessage -Messages $conv.Messages -System $conv.SystemPrompt

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $conv = New-AnthropicConversation -SystemPrompt 'You are a pirate.' Add-AnthropicMessage -Conversation $conv -Role 'user' -Content 'Ahoy!'

maml:description.maml:para: Creates an image content block for the Anthropic Messages API.
Creates a base64-encoded image content block that can be included in messages. Ollama only supports base64 images (not URL-based). Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP
Base64-encoded image data. Must also specify -MediaType.
The MIME type of the image (e.g., 'image/png'). Required when using -Base64.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Path to an image file. The media type is auto-detected from the extension. Supports pipeline input via FullName property (e.g., from Get-ChildItem).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Base64-encoded image data. Must also specify -MediaType.
The MIME type of the image (e.g., 'image/png'). Required when using -Base64.
Path to an image file. The media type is auto-detected from the extension. Supports pipeline input via FullName property (e.g., from Get-ChildItem).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # From file path $imageBlock = New-AnthropicImageContent -Path 'C:\images\screenshot.png'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # From base64 string $imageBlock = New-AnthropicImageContent -Base64 $encodedData -MediaType 'image/jpeg'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Batch process multiple images via pipeline $imageBlocks = Get-ChildItem *.png | New-AnthropicImageContent

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a message hashtable for the Anthropic Messages API.
Creates a properly structured message object for use with Invoke-AnthropicMessage. Messages can contain text content or an array of content blocks (for images, etc.).
The role of the message sender. Must be 'user' or 'assistant'.
The message content. Can be a string or an array of content blocks.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The message content. Can be a string or an array of content blocks.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The role of the message sender. Must be 'user' or 'assistant'.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'user' -Content 'Hello, how are you?' # Creates a simple user message

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: New-AnthropicMessage -Role 'assistant' -Content 'I am doing well, thank you!' # Creates an assistant message (for conversation history)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a tool definition for the Anthropic Tools API.
Creates a properly structured tool definition hashtable that can be passed to Invoke-AnthropicMessage's -Tools parameter for function calling.
The name of the tool. Should be a valid identifier (letters, numbers, underscores).
A description of what the tool does. The model uses this to decide when to call it.
A JSON Schema object defining the tool's parameters. Must be type 'object'.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
A description of what the tool does. The model uses this to decide when to call it.
A JSON Schema object defining the tool's parameters. Must be type 'object'.
The name of the tool. Should be a valid identifier (letters, numbers, underscores).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------

maml:description.maml:para: Auto-generates an Anthropic tool definition from a PowerShell command.
Uses PowerShell reflection to extract parameter metadata from a cmdlet or function and generates an Anthropic-compatible tool definition. This saves you from manually defining JSON schemas for tools.
The function extracts: - Parameter names and types (mapped to JSON Schema types)
- Parameter descriptions from multiple sources (HelpMessage, help content, comments)
- Default values (becomes 'default' in schema)
- Mandatory parameters (become 'required' in schema)
- ValidateSet values (become 'enum' in schema)
- ValidateRange values (become minimum/maximum)
- ValidatePattern (becomes 'pattern')
- ValidateLength (becomes minLength/maxLength)
- ValidateCount (becomes minItems/maxItems for arrays)
- ValidateNotNull/ValidateNotNullOrEmpty (noted in description)
- Parameter aliases (noted in description)
The name of the PowerShell command to convert to a tool definition.
Override the tool description. If not provided, uses the command's Synopsis from Get-Help.
Parameter names to exclude from the tool definition (in addition to common parameters).
Include example values in the schema based on type and validation attributes.
If specified, only include these parameters (still excludes common parameters).
Which parameter set to use when the command has multiple. Defaults to the default parameter set.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Generate strict schema with additionalProperties: false. Recommended for Claude's strict mode.
The name of the PowerShell command to convert to a tool definition.
Override the tool description. If not provided, uses the command's Synopsis from Get-Help.
Parameter names to exclude from the tool definition (in addition to common parameters).
Include example values in the schema based on type and validation attributes.
If specified, only include these parameters (still excludes common parameters).
Which parameter set to use when the command has multiple. Defaults to the default parameter set.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Generate strict schema with additionalProperties: false. Recommended for Claude's strict mode.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Generate tool from a built-in cmdlet $tool = New-AnthropicToolFromCommand -CommandName 'Get-Process'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Generate tool with custom description $tool = New-AnthropicToolFromCommand -CommandName 'Get-ChildItem' -Description 'List files and folders'

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Generate strict tool for Claude's strict mode $tool = New-AnthropicToolFromCommand -CommandName 'Get-Process' -Strict -IncludeExamples

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Generate tool from a custom function function Get-Weather { param( [Parameter(Mandatory, HelpMessage = 'City or region name')] [string]$Location,

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a tool result message to send back to the model.
After the model requests a tool call (stop_reason = 'tool_use'), execute the tool and send the result back using this function. The result is formatted as a user message with tool_result content.
The ID from the tool_use content block in the model's response.
The result of executing the tool. Can be a string or will be converted to JSON.
Indicates the tool execution failed. The content should describe the error.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The result of executing the tool. Can be a string or will be converted to JSON.
Indicates the tool execution failed. The content should describe the error.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
The ID from the tool_use content block in the model's response.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Error case $toolResult = New-AnthropicToolResult -ToolUseId $toolUse.id -Content 'API unavailable' -IsError

maml:description.maml:para: Configures the model router with task-to-model mappings.
Sets up routing rules that map task types to specific models. Also configures logging for routing decisions.
Hashtable mapping task types to model names. Required keys: Default. Optional: Code, Vision, Complex, Fast, Creative.
Path to log file for routing decisions. If not specified, no file logging.
Write routing decisions to console (verbose).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Path to log file for routing decisions. If not specified, no file logging.
Write routing decisions to console (verbose).
Hashtable mapping task types to model names. Required keys: Default. Optional: Code, Vision, Complex, Fast, Creative.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
-------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Test with -WhatIf Set-AnthropicRouterConfig -Models @{ Default = 'llama3' } -LogPath './test.log' -WhatIf

maml:description.maml:para: Tests connectivity to the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
Verifies that the server is reachable and responding. Uses Ollama's root endpoint to check if the server is running.
Server to test. Defaults to the connected server or localhost:11434.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Server to test. Defaults to the connected server or localhost:11434.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-AnthropicEndpoint # Tests the currently connected server

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-AnthropicEndpoint -Server 'localhost:11434' # Tests a specific server
